title: New Horror Movie With 85% Rotten Tomatoes Score May Topple Civil War At The Box Office This Weekend
text: The horror film Abigail is positioned to potentially surpass Civil War at the domestic box office this weekend. According to projections cited by Variety, Abigail could earn between $12-15 million in its debut, while Civil War is expected to bring in $10-15 million. Abigail has received strong early reviews, holding an 85% on Rotten Tomatoes with 41 critics. By comparison, Civil War maintains an 82% critics score and 77% audience approval rating on the same platform. The horror thriller follows a group of criminals who kidnap a young ballerina, only to discover she is secretly a murderous vampire. Directed by Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett, the film stars Kathryn Newton and Dan Stevens and is distributed by Universal Pictures. Civil War achieved a notable $25.7 million opening weekend, setting an A24 distribution record. If Abigail reaches the higher end of its projections, it could become the biggest horror debut of 2024 to date, following the spring success of The First Omen. The film releases in theaters April 19, 2024.
